question_text: A climber hangs from a rope with a mass of 70 kg. The rope can provide a maximum tension of 840 N. If the climber jumps downward and the rope suddenly becomes taut, what is the maximum upward acceleration the rope can provide? (Use g = 10 m/s²)
correct_answer: Maximum upward acceleration = 2 m/s² or 12 m/s² net acceleration relative to gravity
explanation: Weight = 70 × 10 = 700 N downward. Maximum resultant force = 840 − 700 = 140 N upward. Using F = ma, acceleration = 140 ÷ 70 = 2 m/s² upward. This is the net acceleration provided by the rope beyond supporting the climber's weight.
